text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy village at the edge of a vast forest, there lived a curious girl named Elara. Every morning, before she went to school, she would sneak out of her cozy cottage and wander through the dew-covered grass, hoping to discover something new.

Today was no different. The sun peeked through the leaves, painting everything yellow and gold. Elara found herself at the edge of an old oak tree. There was a tiny opening right beside its trunk—a little crack where the bark seemed thinner. She stuck her finger in and pulled a crumpled leaf out. It was wet everywhere.

A gentle tap. She repeated it. Something glinted inside. Carefully, she push her finger further. Silver? Glinted louder. It was a coin! Who could have made it? The coin had an old emblem— a winged lion.
